exemplar.title: To what extent were the Moon administrationâs policies implemented in response to the rising housing prices in the Seoul Metropolitan Area during the COVID-19 pandemic effective in stabilizing the housing market?

detailedExemplarData.title: To what extent were the Moon administrationâs policies implemented in response to the rising housing prices in the Seoul Metropolitan Area during the COVID-19 pandemic effective in stabilizing the housing market?

subject.title: Economics
subject.description:
**IB Economics** is a **Group 3** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **microeconomics, macroeconomics, the global economy, and development economics**, with a strong focus on **analytical and evaluative skills, real-world application of economic theory, and quantitative analysis**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2024**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Extended response questions | 1h 15min | 1h 30min | 30% |
| **Paper 2** | Data response questions | 1h 45min | 1h 45min | 40% (SL), 30% (HL) |
| **Paper 3** | Mathematical questions | N/A | 1h | 20% (HL only) |
| **Internal Assessment** | Portfolio of 3 commentaries | 20 hours | 20 hours | 20% |

strengths: The topic and research question are communicated accurately and effectively with clear focus on specific policies, location, and COVID-19 context.
The research question is concise, focused (âto what extentâ evaluation), and consistently connected to the analysis throughout the essay.
weaknesses: Methodology lacks full justification of data source reliability and limitations.
Methods for measuring policy âeffectivenessâ are not fully explained beyond simple before/after comparisons, indicating partial evidence of informed selection.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The studentâs focused title, clear research question and delimitation to specific policies/timeframe demonstrate strong topic communication and focus (band 5â6). The research question is well stated and consistently addressed (band 5â6). However, the methodology description, while generally relevant, offers limited justification and lacks depth on data reliability and analysis methods, placing it in the top of the 3â4 band. Balancing these performances yields a final holistic score of 5 for Focus and Method.

strengths: Source material is integrated clearly and supports the research narrative (e.g., R-ONE statistics on price surges).
Demonstrates coherent and contextual knowledge of Korean housing economics, applying concepts beyond textbook definitions.
Terminology and economic diagrams are used accurately and consistently, with only minor notation errors that do not impede understanding.
weaknesses: Occasional reliance on secondary commentary instead of first-hand data.
Inconsistent citation formatting (missing years in in-text citations) and minor typographical/formatting errors in quantitative expressions.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Application of source material is mostly relevant but occasionally under-labelled or reliant on secondary commentary, fitting the 3â4 band. Knowledge of the topic is clear and coherent with effective use of sources, meriting 5 (band 5â6). Terminology and concepts are accurate and consistent, meriting 5 (band 5â6). On balance, the overall performance aligns with a final holistic score of 5 for Knowledge and Understanding.

strengths: Research is consistently appropriate and largely relevant, with effective triangulation of official statistics, academic studies, and market data.
Analysis delves into causal chains and supports conclusions with numerical evidence (e.g., supplyâdemand shifts, index changes).
Discussion presents a reasoned argument, evaluating both strengths and weaknesses of policies and reflecting on stakeholder impacts.
weaknesses: Some analytical leaps lack full quantification (e.g., welfare loss estimates are asserted rather than calculated).
Counterfactual reasoning and exploration of alternative policies are limited.
Depth of critical evaluation would benefit from explicit comparisons with other policy options and more rigorous quantification of effects.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The volume and relevance of research justify placement in the 7â9 band, while analysis is clear and mostly supported (4â6 band) and discussion is reasoned but not fully critical (4â6 band). Synthesizing these facets and acknowledging strong research quality leads to an elevated overall score of 8, reflecting solid critical thinking with room to deepen evaluation and counterfactual exploration.

strengths: The essay follows the prescribed EE structure with clear headings, logical sub-sections, and an effective table of contents.
Page numbers, word count, and a title page are correctly included, enhancing navigability and formal compliance.
weaknesses: Figure captions are sometimes truncated and lack complete axis labels.
Long URLs and some tables overrun margins, causing formatting inconsistencies.
Citation formatting varies, and table captions are inconsistently placed, detracting from professional presentation.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Structure is clearly appropriate and adheres to conventions (3â4 band), but noticeable layout and formatting errors (truncated captions, URL breaks, inconsistent citation styles) limit the score to the lower half of the band. Thus, Presentation receives a final score of 3.

strandSelections.reasoning: The research question is reproduced verbatim on the title page and again in the conclusion.  It is concise, focused on âto what extentâ evaluation, names the precise policies, the location (SMA) and the time window (during COVID-19).  Subsequent sections repeatedly return to this question when analysing tax measures and rent cap, showing an effective and sustained connection.  Minor ambiguity remains about the exact definition of âstabilisingâ, so a top-band 6 is not fully justified; 5 is the most fitting score.

The topicâeffectiveness of two specific Moon-administration housing policies in the Seoul Metropolitan Areaâis stated explicitly in both the title and introduction.  The focus on a defined place, time period (COVID-19 years) and two policies reveals a clearly delimited scope that is appropriate for an EE-length investigation.  The introduction links the economic concepts of shortage and demand-pull inflation to the chosen topic, making the purpose and focus transparent.  Minor wordiness is present, but overall communication is accurate and fully appropriate to Economics, meriting placement in the 5-6 band; mid-band 5 best captures some small imprecision in wording.

Data tables, official statistics, and academic literature are woven into each analytical section; figures are cited correctly, and sources clearly relate to the research question.  Nevertheless, some data (e.g., Figure 4 variables) are not fully labelled, and the essay occasionally relies on secondary commentary instead of first-hand data.  Application is thus âmostlyâ rather than âconsistentlyâ relevant, meriting 4 marks in the 3-4 band.

The EE follows the prescribed structure (title page, contents, introduction, methodology, analysis, evaluation, conclusion, references).  Headings are clear, sub-sections logical, and diagrams integrated.  Minor issues include occasional overlap between analysis and evaluation sections, but all required elements are present, meriting the lower half of the 3-4 band (3 marks).

The document displays page numbers, word count, title page and consistent heading styles.  However, figure captions are sometimes truncated, long URLs break line spacing, and some tables overrun page margins.  Citations vary in formatting style.  Layout is therefore âgenerally appropriateâ but with several inaccuracies, fitting 1-2 band; award 2 marks.

The discussion section recognises both strengths and weaknesses of each policy and considers stakeholder impacts, reflecting reasoned argument.  Yet evaluation sometimes lacks depthâthe essay rarely quantifies welfare effects, and alternative policy options are not explored.  The argument reads sequentially rather than being fully synthesised into a critical, well-structured whole.  Hence mid-band (4) is appropriate.

The student constructs supply-and-demand diagrams, interprets index changes and traces causal chains from policy announcement to price movement.  Conclusions about short-term versus long-term effectiveness are usually supported by numerical evidence.  However, some analytical leaps (e.g., precise welfare loss estimation) are asserted rather than calculated, and counterfactual reasoning is limited.  The analysis is therefore âclearâ and âmostly supportedâ, fitting 5 in the 4-6 band.

The student demonstrates solid knowledge of Korean housing economics, distinguishing Jeonse from monthly rent and applying concepts such as shortage, demand-pull inflation and welfare loss.  Explanations are coherent and extend beyond textbook repetition, drawing on contextual data.  Minor oversimplifications (e.g., equating CPI movement directly with housing demand) keep the work slightly below flawless, so 5 within the 5-6 band is appropriate.

A substantial body of official statistics, academic studies and news reports underpins the essay, and almost all of it is directly pertinent to evaluating the two policies.  Source triangulation between R-ONE, KB, KOSIS and scholarly articles shows appropriateness and clear relevance.  Some cited items (e.g., general CPI articles) add limited incremental value, preventing a 9-mark award.  Lower end of the 7-9 band (7) is thus justified.

The student identifies data types (price indices, trade volumes, supplyâdemand indices) and data sources (R-ONE, KOSIS, KB Real Estate) and states use of primary/secondary data.  However, the essay offers only brief justification of why each source was chosen, provides no commentary on reliability/limitations of the data sets, and does not explain methods for measuring policy âeffectivenessâ beyond simple before/after comparison.  The range is generally relevant but evidence of âeffective and informed selectionâ is partial.  This sits most comfortably at the top of the 3-4 band; 4 marks awarded.

Economic terminologyââcapital-gains taxâ, âprice ceilingâ, âlock-in effectâ, âsupply and demand indexââis used accurately and consistently throughout.  Diagrams are correctly labelled with axes, equilibrium points and welfare areas.  Minor typographical errors in variable notation appear but do not impede understanding.  Hence the work meets the descriptors for accurate, consistent use; score 5.

annotations.comment: The title clearly communicates the investigationâs topic and scope, highlighting specific policies, geographic region, and COVID-19 context, demonstrating an appropriately focused research purpose.
Consider refining the title for conciseness by removing extraneous wording; a streamlined title enhances clarity and immediately signals the key variables and context.
The inclusion of a clear word count meeting the 4000-word limit demonstrates adherence to formatting requirements.
The in-text citation â(Kim 46)â lacks publication year; adopt a consistent citation style (e.g., APA or MLA) including author, year, and page number for clarity and academic rigor.
Figure caption could include clear axis titles, units, and a reference date on the graphic itself to strengthen the link between data and interpretation.
Format percentages using LaTeX syntax (e.g., $$1.25\%$$) to maintain consistency and clarity in quantitative expressions.

The research question is concise, clearly focused on âto what extentâ evaluation, and effectively connected to specific policies, place, and time frame, sustaining strong alignment throughout the essay.
The table of contents is comprehensive and logically structured, facilitating easy navigation through major sections and sub-sections.
Consider adding leader dots and consistent alignment between section titles and page numbers to improve readability and professional presentation of the contents.
Effective integration of R-ONE statistics demonstrates clear relevance and supports the narrative of the price surge, showcasing appropriate application of source material.
The methodology section outlines data types and sources but lacks justification of source reliability and limitations; elaborating on data validity would strengthen methodological robustness.
Adjust spacing between section headers and body text to clearly distinguish âShortageâ as a new sub-section and avoid overlap with preceding content.
The description of producer behavior in response to shortage could be refined by explicitly referencing shifts in the supply curve (e.g., $$S \to S_1$$) to align terminology with economic theory.
Place the table caption consistently either above or below tables throughout the essay to maintain uniform formatting and adhere to academic conventions.
Citation â(Lee)â is missing date; include author and year to ensure traceability and consistent referencing.
When defining aggregate demand, include all components in the equation $$AD = C + I + G + NX$$ to provide a comprehensive theoretical basis for demand-pull inflation analysis.
Label the axes clearly with variable names (e.g., price level, real GDP) and designate SRAS, AD, and ADâ curves consistently to enhance interpretability of Figure 3.
Clarify the GDP growth calculation by explicitly converting raw values to a percentage change (e.g., $$\frac{1918.7-1839.5}{1839.5}\times100=4.33\%$$) for analytical rigor.
The discussion linking lower interest rates and fiscal support to increased aggregate demand and housing market vulnerability shows strong critical reasoning and evidence integration.
The application of inflation concepts and linkage to housing as a hedge is well articulated, reflecting clear understanding of macroeconomic effects on the housing market.
In the evaluation of tax measures, consider discussing alternative policies (e.g., direct subsidies or rental support) to demonstrate broader critical evaluation and policy comparison.
The clear definition and application of comprehensive real estate holding tax effectively link policy mechanics to expected market outcomes.
Standardize table source citations by including complete author/date and page details, and ensure punctuation and LaTeX syntax (e.g., $$137$$) are correctly applied.
Quantify the short-term price declines by calculating percentage changes directly in the analysis to deepen insights into the magnitude of policy impact.
Replace the unclear expression â(Q1 - Q_i u)vâ with a precise notation for shortage, such as $$Q_1 - Q_i$$, to avoid confusion and improve mathematical clarity.
